name: Dick Durbin
subject: Add your name: Tell AG Barr to recuse himself
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
#
Peter,
Attorney General Barr's testimony before the Senate Judiciary Committee made it clear that he is more committed to protecting the interests of President Trump than defending the rule of law.
I do not believe that Attorney General Barr should oversee Special Counsel Mueller's 14 remaining criminal referrals. I am calling on him to immediately recuse himself.
**[Enough partisan spin -- the American people want to see justice served. Join me as I call for AG Barr to recuse himself from further investigations related to the Mueller probe.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4d48/42ab3cf4/7cd7d19b/135d61e3/3579076015/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/add-your-name-tell-ag-barr-to-recuse-himself?source=em190507&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
From his biased memo that misconstrued the Special Counsel's report to his false statements and misleading testimony before Congress, the Attorney General has politicized the Mueller investigation and done everything he could to spin its findings to advance the President's political interests.
We cannot allow our Attorney General to act as President Trump's personal attorney and undermine these ongoing criminal matters to protect the President from the consequences.
**[Add your name: Tell AG Barr to recuse himself from all Mueller-related investigations.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4d48/42ab3cf4/7cd7d19b/135d61e3/3579076015/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/add-your-name-tell-ag-barr-to-recuse-himself?source=em190507&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
My best,
Dick Durbin

name: Tennessee Democratic Party
subject: CALL TO ACTION: Demand that your representatives call for Casada's resignation
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
Dear Peter,

Casada must resign now, but leaders like Governor Bill Lee are taking no action - only issuing feeble statements like this:

"When we choose to enter public service, we have an obligation to hold ourselves to a higher standard and cultivate an environment of professionalism and respect. We owe it to Tennesseans to ensure they know that all of us in elected office hold ourselves to that high standard. The recent revelations have shaken that faith, and we need to ensure that confidence is fully restored." - Gov. Bill Lee, in response to Casada scandal

This is how our elected leaders get branded as moral cowards. Governor Bill Lee has a chance to be a real leader & reset the culture at the state capitol, but that would require him to put ambition and politics aside and take a stand.

Across the state, in small towns and big cities, Tennesseans believe in looking out for each other, man, woman, white, black, newcomer or tenth generation. Speaker Glen Casada has spit on and degraded this tradition.

Governor BillLee must search his heart and, if he really cares about a 'higher standard and cultivating an environment of professionalism and respect,' call for Speaker Glen Casada’s resignation.

Since Bill Lee and House Republican members are refusing to take a stand and call for Casada's resignation, we must make them. Here's how:

1) Find your state representative here. http://wapp.capitol.tn.gov/Apps/fmlv3/districts.aspx

2) Call and email them

3) Email Gov. Lee via his contact page or call him at 615-741-2001, and demand he put politics aside and do what's right for Tennessee. https://www.tn.gov/governor/contact-us.html

Sign and share our online petition -- tndp.org/casada -- on social media using the graphic below:

Use this hashtag: #ResignCasada

name: Jeanne Shaheen
subject: Maggie said you might be able to get involved
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Team, I'm about to ask you to pitch in for a one-day fundraising push for our campaign. But first, I want to be really clear about what will happen if we fall short tonight.
**Losing in New Hampshire would mean handing the GOP Senate control for another two years, plain and simple. **Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell want nothing more than to keep confirming ultraconservative judges to the Supreme Court, rolling back health care access and attacking our voting rights -- and they know the best way to do that is to flip this seat red.
**The worst part? CNBC says I 'm one of the two most vulnerable Democratic Senators in the country -- and the GOP knows it.** With the polls showing me tied with right-wing Gov. Sununu, outside groups are now spending more money to defeat me than all the other in-cycle Democrats combined. We're in serious danger of losing this seat -- and losing our chance to stop the Trump-McConnell agenda.
That's why it's so important that grassroots donors like you get involved in this fight now. I know how much you care about taking back the Senate for Democrats, so I'm going to be honest: Missing this goal tonight would put our campaign in serious danger of getting off track.
Look, I need your help. I'm counting on you to help us raise $10,000 before midnight by giving $3 or more now to protect New Hampshire's Senate seat and take back the Senate.
Here's the best link to give: [**https://hassan.bsd.net/before-tonight**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc2f2/2a380d31/382cce0f/f12fff/1620421473/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0Y2pzIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlMjAxOTA1MDhfTVdINENKUzFfUk8iLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4cjs?refcode=e20190508_MWH4CJS1_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250&recurring=auto")
Thank you,
Jeanne

name: Take Action via Lipinski
subject: we can’t just sit by
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LIPINSKI FOR CONGRESS [Click here to unsubscribe from Dan's email list
body:
| | | | | Peter — We need your signature to show that Americans will NOT sit by while polluters pump cancerous chemicals into our air.

[**Please add your name now →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/6610520/60656299/567042405?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOWNhMDM4Mi1hYjcxLWU5MTEtYjQ5ZS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=o69RGtQFY7KfAY-yzKRvbMpJA6aBM4-PCofjG-S6L5A=&emci=9cf1288d-aa71-e911-b49e-281878391efb&emdi=19ca0382-ab71-e911-b49e-281878391efb&ceid=2478574&contactdata=fffap%2FdX6ekpzeBtFdva9P0jPq5Tat9e2DYpHaEzzMuZlmJyXStthu9F1tb7tJ0V1DTx9STsOcVEoS4Zysf5eJm9QfjhtAmd%2FEoPXx%2BF6RUAvoWeUVt6hXCQwUpEGcpJ)

**Begin Forwarded Message:**

Peter,

The American Lung Association released its 2019 report last week and the results are troubling.

More than four in ten Americans are forced to live with unhealthy air quality - and right now the EPA is more focused on cutting regulations than protecting our air.

| We wanted to make sure you saw this urgent message from Ed.
We need to take immediate and transformative action if we're going to save our planet from climate change. Add your name today and join Ed Markey in the fight for a Green New Deal.

**[ADD YOUR NAME](http://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1dd59/693b8b0a/5a3859f4/72deb1dd/1823778600/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://act.edmarkey.com/demand-a-gnd/?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")**
* * *
**The New York Times: Humans Are Speeding Extinction and Altering the Natural World at an 'Unprecedented' Pace**
Team - The latest United Nations study on Earth's biodiversity and the damage being done by global warming paints a disastrous picture for our future.

As many as one million plant and animal species are now at risk of extinction because of climate change and human activity.

In a nutshell: we are literally killing our planet.

We must embrace a Green New Deal. Our future depends on it.

_**[If you agree that we can't wait any longer to combat climate change, then add your name today and demand a Green New Deal.](http://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1dd59/693b8b0a/5a3859f4/72deb1dd/1823778600/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://act.edmarkey.com/demand-a-gnd/?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")**_

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Add your name to support my plan to end the opioid crisis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
The opioid epidemic teaches us that too often in America today, if you have money and power, you can take advantage of everyone else without consequence. I think it's time to change that. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f807/45c30650/86e0757d/6696c782/2174848059/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
In 2017, life expectancy in the United States went _**down**_.

Let me repeat that: Nearly two decades into the 21st century, in the wealthiest country in the history of the world, life expectancy went _**down**_.

And it was largely driven by the opioid epidemic.

Emergency room visits for opioid overdoses have skyrocketed. Children have lost their parents. Parents have lost their children. And only a small percentage of those suffering ever receive the treatment they need.

It's a crisis. A daily emergency -- with severe consequences for communities of color, made worse by the already-wide racial gaps in who gets decent health care and who doesn't.

In 2017, the number of opioid-related deaths in Baltimore was almost as high as West Virginia, the state with the highest death rate in the country -- with most death occurring in the black community.

This isn't the first time we've faced a huge public health crisis -- **and when we look at our history, we see a path to end the opioid epidemic.

Today, in partnership with Representative Elijah Cummings, I'm rolling out the CARE Act -- a comprehensive plan to end the opioid crisis by providing the resources needed to begin treating this epidemic like the public health emergency that it is.**

It's modeled after the Ryan White CARE Act, which finally provided significant new, guaranteed funding to help state and local governments combat the HIV/AIDS epidemic and established a safety net for those living with the disease.

The CARE Act provides resources directly to first responders, public health departments, and communities on the front lines of this crisis for prevention, treatment, and recovery services.

It's $100 billion in federal funding over the next 10 years -- because that's what's needed to make sure every single person gets the treatment they need. And don't worry, we already know how to pay for it.

**[Please add your name to join the fight against the opioid epidemic and become a citizen co-sponsor of the CARE Act today.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f807/45c30650/86e0757d/6696c785/2174848059/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUwOGVtMS1mdWxsLWEifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-opioid-epidemic?source=20190508em1-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

This week, I'll visit Kermit, West Virginia, a small town located along the Kentucky-West Virginia border. Kermit has been hard-hit by the opioid epidemic -- and not by accident.

In just a few years, this town of only 400 people was inundated with 13 million prescription opioid pills, all delivered to a single local pharmacy -- that's more than _30,000 pills per resident_. The companies shipping these pills repeatedly disregarded requirements to report suspicious patterns of behavior, and the state Board of Pharmacy failed to enforce its own rules.

Now, Kermit is fighting back with a lawsuit against the pharmacy and five wholesale drug distributors that exacerbated the crisis. It's just one of many similar lawsuits lodged by towns, counties, and states across the country -- and I hope they win. But the fact that any of this happened in the first place speaks to something rotten in our system.

**Here 's the truth: big business is fueling addiction.** The five companies that Kermit is suing earned $17 billion shipping prescription opioids to West Virginia during the period in question, and their CEOs took home millions in bonuses and pay.

This crisis has been driven by greed, pure and simple. Just look at the Sackler family. They own a pharmaceutical empire. They're billionaires. They own mansions around the world. Entire wings of museums in New York and London have been stamped with the family name.

But here's the thing: the Sacklers made their money pushing OxyContin. Pushing it even as study after study demonstrated its addictive potential. Even as hundreds of thousands of Americans died. And how did the Sackler family react? They tried to increase their profits by opening a network of for-profit recovery centers to treat the very same health crisis they were fueling.

**The opioid epidemic teaches us that too often in America today, if you have money and power, you can take advantage of everyone else without consequence. I think it 's time to change that.**

Under my opioid plan, billionaires like the Sacklers wouldn't get to live the high life while only 1 out of 5 folks who need opioid treatment get the help they need. Instead, they would pay up to help make sure every person gets the care they need. And under my Corporate Executive Accountability Act, executives of major companies that deliberately hurt people through criminal negligence -- for example, by dumping mountains of highly addictive pills into towns like Kermit to make a quick buck -- don't just pay a fine, they face real criminal penalties.

**Real, structural change to address this crisis is going to take new leadership in Washington. Leadership that will hold business executives that cheat and defraud and addict people responsible for their criminal acts. Leadership that requires folks like the Sacklers and their heirs to pay a bit more to clean up the mess they caused.**

That's the America we deserve. An America where we take care of each other, where health care for every person who needs it matters more than rich families shielding their wealth. An America where when people like the Sacklers destroy millions of lives to make money, they don't get museum wings named after them, they go to jail.

**[If you're with me in this fight to end the opioid epidemic and make sure a crisis like this never happens again, add your name here:](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f807/45c30650/86e0757d/6696c785/2174848059/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUwOGVtMS1mdWxsLWEifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-opioid-epidemic?source=20190508em1-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

**[https://my.elizabethwarren.com/opioid-plan](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f807/45c30650/86e0757d/6696c784/2174848059/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUwOGVtMS1mdWxsLWEifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/opioid-plan?source=20190508em1-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth
| [**SIGN THE PETITION**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f807/45c30650/86e0757d/6696c785/2174848059/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUwOGVtMS1mdWxsLWEifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-opioid-epidemic?source=20190508em1-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")

| | Hi,

We’ve all been working hard over the past few months to make sure we come out on top on Primary Day. But none of it will matter if our supporters don’t show up at the polls.

So I’m asking: Will you promise to turn out on May 21 and vote for Adam and me?

[**Click here to add your name to our pledge and let us know if we can count on your vote on Primary Day.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/6614671/60688795/-1069522434?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MjYxNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NmY4MTM3Mi1jMTcxLWU5MTEtYjQ5ZS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SNCqnkm1K6IUuE0o1zm3bRC2O3H224YzZp91F43YWJ8=&emci=be9a3729-bb71-e911-b49e-281878391efb&emdi=86f81372-c171-e911-b49e-281878391efb&ceid=7712083&contactdata=hoaj9OJCjfzQT4xFYBjQ%2F%2BjupR8c%2FlW3YBvKmxwEdqFDGiqmWgKwFxbKWRSFkXoi)

You and I both know that our team offers the best way forward for our Commonwealth. Adam and I are running for one reason and one reason only—to make life better for all Kentuckians.

Our focus is on creating high-quality jobs, providing opportunity to all, protecting people’s health care and making sure that our elected officials are held accountable.

Tuesday, May 21, is our first big hurdle on the road to the governor’s office, but we’re going to need your help to clear it!

[**Please take a moment to sign our pledge and tell us that you’re committed to coming out and voting for Adam and me on Primary Day.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/6614672/60688796/-1069522434?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MjYxNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NmY4MTM3Mi1jMTcxLWU5MTEtYjQ5ZS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SNCqnkm1K6IUuE0o1zm3bRC2O3H224YzZp91F43YWJ8=&emci=be9a3729-bb71-e911-b49e-281878391efb&emdi=86f81372-c171-e911-b49e-281878391efb&ceid=7712083&contactdata=hoaj9OJCjfzQT4xFYBjQ%2F%2BjupR8c%2FlW3YBvKmxwEdqFDGiqmWgKwFxbKWRSFkXoi)

Thanks for your support,

Gill

| | |
# **First Lady Melania Trump Announces Expansion of Her ‘Be Best’ Initiative with Focus on Child Welfare and Drug Abuse**

“First Lady Melania Trump marked the anniversary of her ‘Be Best’ campaign on Tuesday by announcing the expansion of the initiative and another international trip,” Andrew O’Reilly reports for Fox News.

The First Lady’s “Be Best” initiative “will now include children of all ages, not just babies born dependent on opioids and other addictive substances, and young mothers. The social media pillar will broaden to add online safety to the emphasis on bullying and good citizenship online.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7b6decefad&e=3ee4985eea)

**🎬****First Lady:** [“Be Best” has one goal—to educate children and parents](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f7929624a1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| Today, President Donald J. Trump travels to Panama City, Florida, where he will receive a briefing on ongoing recovery efforts at Tyndall Air Force Base, the _[News Herald](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fb0a588e58&e=3ee4985eea)_ reports. “The eye of Hurricane Michael passed directly over Tyndall Air Force Base, leaving the structures devastated and the base’s capabilities compromised”—but the U.S. Air Force intends to rebuild Tyndall into a “base of the future” once Congressional Democrats stop obstructing the funding.
---
|
---
| “House Democrats are escalating their campaign against the Trump Administration with complaints that its resistance to Congressional requests for documents is a threat to democracy . . . The real offense against the Constitution here is by Democrats,” _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f76436cbc2&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “The current pseudo-impeachment is a fraud on the Constitution and the American public.”
---
|
---
| “President Trump on Sunday announced additional incoming tariffs on China, reminding Beijing that its days of negotiating with weak counterparts are over, at least as far as it concerns the United States,” Christian Whiton writes in [Fox Business](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=842f1589ed&e=3ee4985eea). “Rather than cave in or walk away, Trump has cranked up the pressure on China . . . Either they shape up or face the consequences of more economic pain, as they help fill the coffers of the U.S. Treasury through higher tariffs.”
---
|
---
| “The Department of Veterans Affairs is achieving record levels of transparency, which is a vital step to improving care for veterans at VA nursing home facilities,” Veterans Affairs Secretary Robert Wilkie writes in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fafb743897&e=3ee4985eea)_. “These unprecedented improvements, and the many strides we are making to hold ourselves accountable and put our Veterans first, are all part of enacting President Trump’s promise to correct the problems of the past.”
---

name: Kamala Harris
subject: Your invitation to Camp Kamala
body:
Just yesterday, we launched Camp Kamala, a grassroots program to empower
our supporters to organize in their communities. The response so far has
been truly amazing. In the last 24 hours alone, thousands have signed up
to get involved -- people in all 50 states, Puerto Rico, Armed Forces
Europe and Armed Forces Pacific!

Our organizing team is building a unique national volunteer program that
will engage and train leaders in every corner of our country on the work
it’s going to take to win this election, and I want you to be a part of
it. You can read more in our kickoff message below.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-org-190506-camp-kamala?refcode=ema190508-full&source=ema190508-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1001&akid=4664%2E5755789%2ExsDvEH ]But first, I want to take a moment to personally invite you to join our
movement today and attend our very first Camp Kamala session. Sign up here
and let’s get to work.

For The People,

— Kamala Harris

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Original Message from Jose Nunez, May 7, 2019

Howdy!

I’m reaching out to share some really important news: we’re opening up
enrollment for an awesome program called Camp Kamala, and I want you to
sign up to join us.

It’s sort of like summer camp, but better: Camp Kamala will be all about
how we elect Kamala the next President of the United States.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-org-190506-camp-kamala?refcode=ema190508-full&source=ema190508-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1002&akid=4664%2E5755789%2ExsDvEH ]If you’re already on board, click here to sign up today and follow
instructions to join our online community and register for the first
training!

Camp Kamala will empower supporters to join our movement, no matter where
you live. Our eight-week online program will teach you the ins and outs of
volunteering for our campaign, including a crash course in the
cutting-edge tools we’re using to spread our message and get Kamala
elected. Then, you’ll be all trained as a volunteer leader for Kamala in
your community.

Don’t know how much time you can commit yet? No worries. After the first
few sessions, you’ll be able to pick one of two “tracks” of the program:
Camp Kamala and Camp Kamala+.

Camp Kamala

* Campers will attend one online training session per week, followed by
a quiz at the end of each training session
* After Camp is over, you’ll be fully trained and empowered to lead
community and volunteer events in your community for Kamala

Camp Kamala+

* An accelerated track that provides additional opportunities for
volunteer leadership, including the ability to train other volunteers
* Campers will attend additional online training sessions per week and
complete one homework assignment per week

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-org-190506-camp-kamala?refcode=ema190508-full&source=ema190508-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1003&akid=4664%2E5755789%2ExsDvEH ]Sign up to join Camp Kamala today and start your training to become a
volunteer leader in your community!

Talk more soon,

— Jose

Jose Nunez
Digital Organizing Director
Kamala 2020

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://action.kamalaharris.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=4664%2E5755789%2ExsDvEH

name: Dan Baer
subject: Standing up to the NRA together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
I grew up four minute drive or 10 minute bike ride from Columbine.
|
---

| | I know you get a lot of these emails, but I want to talk about the NRA, Cory Gardner, and the ways you can help stop the epidemic of gun violence in Colorado, and in our country.

I grew up in Colorado just like Cory Gardner. But unlike him, I grew up four minute drive or 10 minute bike ride from Columbine. That Senator Gardner continues to do nothing to combat the epidemic of gun violence in our country is unconscionable. _**Unlike him, I 've been supporting common sense gun safety for my whole adult life.**_

Back in 1999, as a senior in college, I wrote in The Christian Science Monitor that "when National Rifle Association members self-righteously toss out arguments about 'freedom,' my generation should react with justifiable indignation. What about the freedom of a 14-year-old to feel safe at school?"

And the other month, I lamented on MSNBC to Andrea Mitchell that "it's been too long... there are too many people who have gone through too much pain... this is a choice that we're making to not deal with the epidemic of gun violence."

Senator Gardner's inaction on this issue is a choice -- he could be the deciding vote in the Senate on gun safety. I'm running because I want to be that deciding vote and I want to vote for common sense measures like the bipartisan bill on background checks that passed the House this year but are stuck without a vote in the Senate. Cory Gardner's votes are dictated by the NRA rather than by the public health and safety needs of our children and our country.

Recently, the NRA's president claimed that Lucy McBath, mother of Jordan Davis -- a young man who was shot and killed -- only won her race in the Georgia 6th because she was black. That's racist. And it's wrong. Lucy McBath won because she was a mother who was moved to take the tragedy of her son's death as inspiration to make change happen.

Once upon a time the NRA really was on the side of gun safety. Today it has lost its way, and its leadership is willing to stand against logic, compassion, common sense, and the genuine public safety needs of our country. _**We cannot allow this organization to continue to exercise its outsized role in American politics.**_ Now, the NRA has been in the news plenty recently. They're in turmoil. There are financial issues in the organization. They're leaderless. _**But make no mistake, a wounded tiger is the most dangerous kind of tiger. The NRA is still one of the most formidable entities in American politics.**_ The gun violence prevention policies I would support in the US Senate represent a clear and present danger to the NRA and their priorities -- they are going to try to drown our campaign in PAC money.

That's part of why I'm working so hard, and why I continue to ask for support.

We can stand against the NRA and turn back the tide of senseless violence on our country, but we can only do it together. I know that I certainly can't do this without you, so I'm asking you to contribute today so that we can stand against the NRA and their wrongheaded policies. | | [**Donate $19 now >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb8269c/232dfd1/919a675/62a1515c/2347171534/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190508_em_fr_b1_baer_all&amount=19&recurring=on")

Thank you so much for you support,
Dan

The NRA is still one of the most formidable entities in American politics. The gun violence prevention policies I would support in the US Senate represent a clear and present danger to the NRA and their priorities -- they are going to try to drown our campaign in PAC money. That's part of why I'm working so hard, and why I continue to ask for support. We can stand against the NRA and turn back the tide of senseless violence on our country, but we can only do it together. I know that I certainly can't do this without you, so I'm asking you to contribute today so that we can stand against the NRA and their wrongheaded policies. | | "" "" "" "" Thank you so much for you support, Dan | "" "" Dan for Colorado PO Box 1199 Denver, CO 80201 "" | | Paid for by Dan for Colorado

name: Kirsten Gillibrand 2020
subject: Reproductive rights are nonnegotiable
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020
body:
I will always defend a woman's right to make her own personal health decisions, including accessing abortion.
| | |
---
Reproductive rights are civil rights, and they are nonnegotiable. I will always defend a woman's right to make her own personal health decisions, including accessing abortion.
Peter, this is going to be a long email--but I hope you'll read through, because we're living in a time when reproductive rights are under attack like never before. The anti-choice rhetoric of Donald Trump and Mike Pence and the extremists they've put on the Supreme Court have emboldened Republicans at the state level to escalate their assault on abortion access and reproductive health care.
**I believe in my bones that I am the candidate who will best fight to protect reproductive freedom from these attacks. If you're committed to this fight,[can you donate to my campaign? I still need to reach the 65,000-donor threshold to guarantee my spot on the Democratic debate stage, where I'll be the loudest voice for women's rights.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f97724d/2ae112ef/6ff25e33/357c5f7b/448493649/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMwOTctZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3097-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
| | [DONATE](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f97724d/2ae112ef/6ff25e33/357c5f7b/448493649/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMwOTctZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3097-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")
---
My top priority throughout my career has been fighting for the rights of women as full and equal members of society. We simply can't accomplish that without the guarantee of reproductive freedom. That's why I announced yesterday that I will only nominate judges--including Supreme Court justices--who will commit to upholding _Roe v. Wade_ and protecting reproductive rights.
Today, I want to expand a bit on the rest of my reproductive rights platform. It's made up of four key tenets:
**1\. ACCESS: I will do everything in my power to protect access to safe, legal abortion.** 73% of Americans support _Roe v. Wade_ , and women deserve a president who will protect their basic right to make personal health decisions. We have to reject politicians' efforts to chip away at reproductive health care access in Congress and in states across the country. I would end harmful policies like the Hyde Amendment, which disproportionately restricts access to abortion for low-income women and women of color. And I would repeal President Trump's gag rule, because no politician should come between doctors and their patients.
**2\. PREVENTION: I will protect access to health care that prevents unintended pregnancy.** Let's state the obvious: If politicians really wanted to decrease abortion rates, rather than just interfere in women's lives, they'd get serious about defending and expanding access to birth control, guaranteeing sex education, and investing in--not gutting--Title X family planning funding. This is basic health care, and we won't go backward on it.
**3\. CARE: I will protect doctors and organizations that provide abortion --not defund, silence, criminalize or endanger them****.** The lies Donald Trump peddles about abortion providers put them and their patients in grave danger. We must put a stop to clinic violence by protecting the doctors, nurses and clinic workers who provide abortion care. And I will always protect funding for Planned Parenthood, a lifesaving, necessary health care provider for millions of Americans.
**4\. COURTS: I will protect the courts from being taken over by anti-choice extremists.** I announced yesterday that as president, I will only nominate judges--including Supreme Court justices--who will commit to upholding _Roe v. Wade_ as settled law and protect women's reproductive rights. With Republicans waging an unprecedented assault on abortion access, the judiciary is one of our most crucial lines of defense--which is why I'm drawing a hard line that any judges I appoint will defend women's health and rights.
Americans are guaranteed the constitutional right to legal abortion in _Roe v. Wade_ , and it's past time for Republicans to stop using the issue as a political football. This isn't a game. Women's lives are on the line.
Our right to make personal health decisions for ourselves and our families is nonnegotiable. In the face of relentless attacks from the Trump administration and its allies in Congress, I have always stood up to protect women's access to safe and legal abortion, and I'll continue to do so as president.
**I will be a voice on the Democratic debate stage for abortion rights and reproductive freedom. But I need your help to get there:[Donate right now to help me reach the 65,000-donor threshold I need to guarantee my spot at the debates, and join the team fighting for reproductive freedom.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f97724d/2ae112ef/6ff25e33/357c5f7b/448493649/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMwOTctZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3097-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**

name: Andrew Yang
subject: The Campaign as Family
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05082019_newsletter_2&n=1) We were in Des Moines the other day wrapping up a town hall. It was Zach, Carly, Luke, and I. It was raining and chilly. Our white Chevy Suburban was parked far across the parking lot. We looked at each other for a second. Were we all going to go to the car in the rain? Or was someone going to run to the car and drive it over for the rest of us? Zach quickly said, “I’ll run out and get the car,” and then dashed into the rain while the rest of us waited. After he pulled up, we piled in. Carly said, “That was such a dad move, Zach.” It’s true. That’s the sort of thing I do with my family all of the time. I go and get the car in harsh elements while they wait under shelter. So then I said, “Wait a minute. If Zach is the Dad of this family, what am I?” And then it dawned on me—I'm the baby. I realized a bunch of things very quickly:
* People are taking care of me all of the time.
* Staffers have my favorite foods around.
* If I get in a bad mood, everyone around me suffers.
* My schedule determines everyone else’s.
* Others are grooming me, sometimes several times a day.

The parallels were eerie. Zach is the patient Dad. Carly, the energetic sister. Luke is the quiet son. And I am the kid around which the world revolves. It is hard to understand how close you get on the campaign trail. You literally wake up together in an Airbnb, eat together, ride together in a car for hours on end, and coordinate every part of your day. It is very intense. You are in tight proximity to each other for hours and hours. I’ve noticed that some other candidates travel with actual family members for this reason. I’m glad to say that the team and I enjoy our time on the road, even when it gets tiring. We get along famously. Good thing, because we travel a lot. I’m incredibly grateful to the people on the campaign team. Most joined before I had any level of public recognition. They saw something in me and my ideas. Many took huge pay cuts because they saw the chance to have a huge impact. Their talent level and idealism are both sky high. This campaign attracts amazing people. I’m accustomed to being the father figure—the CEO of a business, the head of a non-profit, and, of course, being an actual dad. I hope to be the leader of a revolution that transforms the country. But there are many times on the campaign where I’m the person being taken care of instead of the opposite. And I’m grateful for it. Let someone else run into the rain. name: Andrew Romanoff
subject: Highlands Ranch
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROMANOFF FOR COLORADO
body:
Nearly every day in America brings a mass shooting, followed by a flurry of words to distract us. These words don't comfort grieving parents. They're largely empty arguments; they change nothing.. |
---
| | | "Now is not the time." "Guns don't kill people." "Laws don't stop criminals." "It's a slippery slope." "It's the video games." "Violent movies." "Broken homes." Nearly every day in America brings a mass shooting, followed by a flurry of words to distract us. These words don't comfort grieving parents. They're largely empty arguments; they change nothing. Today we mourn a victim, salute his bravery, and console his family. We comfort the survivors at STEM School Highlands Ranch, including those who risked their lives to protect others. We thank our law enforcement officers and first responders, who take such risks every day. The best way to honor these heroes is to make their heroism unnecessary. That means, among other things, doing what we can to keep firearms from falling into the wrong hands. Colorado took a step in that direction this year by creating extreme risk protection orders. Red-flag laws like these reduce the risk of violence--chiefly suicide, which accounts for most gun deaths in America. What would have prevented yesterday's tragedy in Highlands Ranch? It's both too late to act and too soon to say. But we can make such tragedies less likely by enacting common-sense measures like universal background checks--and, yes, by enforcing the laws we have, including prohibitions on straw purchases. Enforcement requires resources, which the gun lobby and its allies have taken pains to restrict. There's one point the gun lobby gets right: no law will save every life. But that's no excuse for saving none. Andrew Romanoff

subject: Why Americans hate the swamp
body:
Today is an example of why Congress' approval rating is 20 percent. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6fa860be15&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • May 8, 2019
---
| | |
# **Why Americans hate the swamp**

**Today** is an example of why Congress' approval rating is 20 percent.

**It doesn't take a polling expert** to tell you that a majority of Americans want Washington to work on solving problems that make their lives better, not on advancing their own Beltway careers through noisy, never-ending spectacles of partisan theater.

**Americans know the drill** by now: Careerist politicians yell. They hold hearings. They call on witnesses. They make themselves look busy. And then they do nothing of actual substance to help the very people who elected them in the first place.

**No ordinary citizen** thinks for a second that Congressional Democrats are making a principled stand for a Constitution they readily ignored through eight years of Obama Administration executive overreach. Voters see how the political establishment treats the Constitution as a tool of convenience—useful to praise at times, then easily ignored when it gets in the way. Everyone knows today’s performance is about one thing only: power.

**Congressional Democrats** don't even pretend to care about governing anymore. Not fixing our broken immigration system. Not rebuilding infrastructure. Not jobs. Not trade. Not manufacturing. Not anything the American people want and need Washington to help fix.

**Press Secretary Sarah Sanders** released a statement today in response to House Democrats’ ongoing refusal to accept the conclusions of the Mueller Report—and, by extension, their refusal to accept the legitimacy of the 2016 election. It reads in part:
| | |
---|---|---
| _The American people see through Chairman Nadler’s desperate ploy to distract from the President’s historically successful agenda and our booming economy. Neither the White House nor Attorney General Barr will comply with Chairman Nadler’s unlawful and reckless demands . . . The American people deserve a Congress that is focused on solving real problems like the crisis at the border, high prescription drug prices, our country’s crumbling infrastructure, and so much more._ |

**Donald J. Trump** is President largely because Americans couldn’t stand another minute of Washington’s do-nothing, business-as-usual, insider political circus. Today, in the halls of the United States Congress, the swamp proved that it hasn’t listened to a single word of it.

[See the Press Secretary’s full statement.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=17301bbcf8&e=3ee4985eea)
|
---
|
## **One year after leaving the disastrous Iran deal**

**One year ago,** President Trump officially withdrew the United States from the fatally flawed, one-sided Iran nuclear deal—under which Iran was free to sponsor terrorist networks, develop its missile force, and brutalize its own people. Today, the President imposed tough new sanctions to further deprive Iran of funds for its campaign of violent terror.

**“We are successfully imposing** the most powerful maximum pressure campaign ever witnessed, which today’s action will further strengthen,” President Trump said in a statement. “Tehran can expect further actions unless it fundamentally alters its conduct.”

[See President Trump’s official statement on Iran.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0b955c43b0&e=3ee4985eea)

**Get the facts:** [America is cutting off the Iranian regime’s cash flow ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4bae9bd65d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: President Trump heads to Air Force base in Florida**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=930fdbe449&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **This afternoon,** President Trump took off for Panama City, Florida, to visit the Tyndall Air Force Base that was ravaged by Hurricane Michael last year. Despite the base seeing most of its structures damaged from the storm, the United States Air Force is determined to build Tyndall into the “base of the future.”

**For National Hurricane Preparedness Week,** President Trump is encouraging all Americans who reside in hurricane-prone areas to safeguard their families and homes.

**“As a Nation** , we are sadly too familiar with the destructive force of hurricanes,” the President said in his proclamation. “To help prevent and mitigate hurricane-related harm, it is critically important that we prepare for the upcoming hurricane season, which starts next month.”

**One helpful tool** the President called attention to this week is [the Ready Campaign](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1eefed4229&e=3ee4985eea). Led by FEMA, the campaign is an online resource for all disaster planning needs.

[See President Trump’s Proclamation on National Hurricane Preparedness Week.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e7cb1f5591&e=3ee4985eea)
---

No ordinary citizen thinks for a second that Congressional Democrats are making a principled stand for a Constitution they readily ignored through eight years of Obama Administration executive overreach. Voters see how the political establishment treats the Constitution as a tool of convenience—useful to praise at times, then easily ignored when it gets in the way. Everyone knows today’s performance is about one thing only: power. Congressional Democrats don't even pretend to care about governing anymore. Not fixing our broken immigration system. Not rebuilding infrastructure. Not jobs. Not trade. Not manufacturing. Not anything the American people want and need Washington to help fix. Press Secretary Sarah Sanders released a statement today in response to House Democrats’ ongoing refusal to accept the conclusions of the Mueller Report—and, by extension, their refusal to accept the legitimacy of the 2016 election. It reads in part: | | | ---|---|--- | The American people see through Chairman Nadler’s desperate ploy to distract from the President’s historically successful agenda and our booming economy. Neither the White House nor Attorney General Barr will comply with Chairman Nadler’s unlawful and reckless demands . . . The American people deserve a Congress that is focused on solving real problems like the crisis at the border, high prescription drug prices, our country’s crumbling infrastructure, and so much more. | Donald J. Trump is President largely because Americans couldn’t stand another minute of Washington’s do-nothing, business-as-usual, insider political circus. Today, in the halls of the United States Congress, the swamp proved that it hasn’t listened to a single word of it. | | One year after leaving the disastrous Iran deal One year ago, President Trump officially withdrew the United States from the fatally flawed, one-sided Iran nuclear deal—under which Iran was free to sponsor terrorist networks, develop its missile force, and brutalize its own people. Today, the President imposed tough new sanctions to further deprive Iran of funds for its campaign of violent terror. “We are successfully imposing the most powerful maximum pressure campaign ever witnessed, which today’s action will further strengthen,” President Trump said in a statement. “Tehran can expect further actions unless it fundamentally alters its conduct.” Get the facts: | | Video of the day: President Trump heads to Air Force base in Florida | | This afternoon, President Trump took off for Panama City, Florida, to visit the Tyndall Air Force Base that was ravaged by Hurricane Michael last year. Despite the base seeing most of its structures damaged from the storm, the United States Air Force is determined to build Tyndall into the “base of the future.” For National Hurricane Preparedness Week, President Trump is encouraging all Americans who reside in hurricane-prone areas to safeguard their families and homes. “As a Nation , we are sadly too familiar with the destructive force of hurricanes,” the President said in his proclamation. “To help prevent and mitigate hurricane-related harm, it is critically important that we prepare for the upcoming hurricane season, which starts next month.” One helpful tool the President called attention to this week is . name: Bernie Sanders
subject: It is unacceptable for the president to declare executive privilege in response to subpoenas about the Mueller report. Congress — and the public — deserve to know the full truth.

body:
What is he trying to hide?
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1295%2E150750%2ErbusM6)

Friends -
President Trump’s Justice Department just took an unprecedented action and denied a lawful request from the House of Representatives asking for unredacted documents relating to the Mueller report.
They are exerting “executive privilege,” declaring that they will not respond to any subpoenas.
President Trump seems determined to engineer a constitutional crisis. His refusal to comply with a standard congressional subpoena is completely unacceptable, especially because he has been insisting for weeks that the Mueller report fully exonerates him. If that is true, then why is he trying to block lawmakers from simply reading the full report? What is he trying to hide?
One of the most fundamental jobs of members of Congress is to oversee the executive branch of the federal government. Lawmakers have a right and an obligation to read the entire Mueller report in order to perform those oversight responsibilities.
The report did not reach a conclusion on whether President Trump and his administration obstructed justice — and that means it is especially important for Congress to thoroughly investigate Mueller’s full findings. We cannot do that if the president blocks access to the report.
As Donald Trump has said before, “Let it come out, let the people see it." I couldn’t agree more. It is unacceptable for the president to declare executive privilege in response to these subpoenas. Congress — and the public — deserve to know the full truth.
Donald Trump may think he is a dictator — but he is not. He is not above the law and he is not above the constitution.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
